title: Authors and Citation
Authors and Citation
text: Dall'Olio F (2024). butlerrr: Helper Convenience Functions Recurring Coding Operations. R package version 0.1.0, https://f-dallolio.github.io/butlerrr/, https://github.com/f-dallolio/butlerrr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {butlerrr: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations},   author = {Filippo Dall'Olio},   year = {2024},   note = {R package version 0.1.0, https://f-dallolio.github.io/butlerrr/},   url = {https://github.com/f-dallolio/butlerrr}, }

what: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations
title: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations
text: goal butlerrr provide helper convenience (hence name) functions recurring R coding operations.

title: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations
text: can install development version butlerrr GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("f-dallolio/butlerrr")

title: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations
text: Butlerrr provides helper convenience functions help coding manipulation different kinds R objects. important functions butlerrr deal symbolic objects calls. Specifically, symc (alias as_symbolic_obj) encall, well vectorized equivalents symcs (as_symbolic_objs) encalls, convert arbitrary R objects symbolic objects (symbols calls) calls.

title: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations
text: exaples symbolic objects symc (as_symbolic_obj)… …vectorized version symcs (as_symbolic_objs). symcs (as_symbolic_objs) also include argument called .x vector inputs gets appended elements ... arguments naming. example, .named TRUE automatically names unnamed elements resulting list.
code: library(butlerrr) symc("mean") # string representation of a symbol returns a symbol #> mean symc("mean()") # string representation of a call returns a call #> mean() symc(mean) # a function returns the call including the function namespace. #> base::mean(x, ...) x <- list("sd", 1) symcs("mean", "mean()", mean) #> [[1]] #> mean #>  #> [[2]] #> mean() #>  #> [[3]] #> base::mean(x, ...) symcs("mean", b = "mean()", mean, .x = x, .named = TRUE) #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $b #> mean() #>  #> $`base::mean(x, ...)` #> base::mean(x, ...) #>  #> $sd #> sd #>  #> $`1` #> `1`

title: Helper and Convenience Functions for Recurring Coding Operations
text: encall encalls convert objects “named” calls (e.g. form func(args)).
code: encall("mean") #> mean() encall("mean()") #> mean() encall(mean) #> base::mean(x, ...) x <- list("sd", 1) encalls("mean", "mean()", mean) #> [[1]] #> mean() #>  #> [[2]] #> mean() #>  #> [[3]] #> base::mean(x, ...) encalls("mean", b = "mean()", mean, .x = x, .named = TRUE) #> $`mean()` #> mean() #>  #> $b #> mean() #>  #> $`base::mean(x, ...)` #> base::mean(x, ...) #>  #> $`sd()` #> sd() #>  #> $``1`()` #> `1`()

title: Convert Object(s) to Symbolic(s) — as-symbolic
text: functions take various R objects return equivalent symbols orcalls (e.g. "func" func, "pkg::func(x, ...)" pkg::func(x, ...), etc.`). as_symbolic_obj() returns symbolic. as_symbolic_objs() returns list symbolics length input greater one. symbolic otherwise.

title: Convert Object(s) to Symbolic(s) — as-symbolic
code: as_symbolic_obj(x, with_ns = TRUE)  as_symbolic_objs(   ...,   .x = NULL,   .named = FALSE,   .name_all = FALSE,   .names_type = c("full", "sym", "head") )  symc(x, with_ns = TRUE)  symcs(   ...,   .x = NULL,   .named = FALSE,   .name_all = FALSE,   .names_type = c("full", "sym", "head") )

title: Convert Object(s) to Symbolic(s) — as-symbolic
text: x R object. Precisely, syntactic literal, symbol, call, function, formula. ... R objects . .x list R objects. gets appended .... .named TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), unnamed elements output as_symbolic_objs automatically named. .name_all TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), elements output as_symbolic_objs automatically named. .names_type one c("full", "sym", "head"). "full" output equivalent deparse(x). "sym" function returns string equivalent symbol x symbol call arguments. "head" function returns string equivalent symbol call name.

title: Convert Object(s) to Symbolic(s) — as-symbolic
Convert Object(s) to Symbolic(s) — as-symbolic
code: as_symbolic_obj(quote(mean)) #> mean as_symbolic_obj("mean") #> mean as_symbolic_obj("base::mean(1:10)") #> base::mean(1:10) as_symbolic_obj(quote(sd(1:10))) #> sd(1:10) as_symbolic_obj(quote(base::mean)) #> base::mean  as_symbolic_objs(quote(mean), "mean", "base::mean(1:10)", quote(sd(1:10))) #> [[1]] #> mean #>  #> [[2]] #> mean #>  #> [[3]] #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> [[4]] #> sd(1:10) #>   x <- list(quote(mean), b = "mean", c = "base::mean(1:10)", quote(sd(1:10))) as_symbolic_objs("fn", .x = x) #> [[1]] #> fn #>  #> [[2]] #> mean #>  #> $b #> mean #>  #> $c #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> [[5]] #> sd(1:10) #>  as_symbolic_objs("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE) #> $fn #> fn #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $b #> mean #>  #> $c #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  as_symbolic_objs("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE) #> $fn #> fn #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $`base::mean(1:10)` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  as_symbolic_objs("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE, .names_type = "full") #> $fn #> fn #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $`base::mean(1:10)` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  as_symbolic_objs("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE, .names_type = "sym") #> $fn #> fn #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $`base::mean(1:10)` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  as_symbolic_objs("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE, .names_type = "head") #> $fn #> fn #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $mean #> mean #>  #> $`base::mean` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $sd #> sd(1:10) #>

title: Convert Object(s) to Call(s) — encall
text: functions take various R objects return equivalent named calls (e.g. func(x, ...), pkg::func(x, ...), etc.). encall() returns named call. encalls() returns list named calls.

title: Convert Object(s) to Call(s) — encall
code: encall(x)  encalls(..., .x = NULL, .named = FALSE, .name_all = FALSE, .names_type = NULL)

title: Convert Object(s) to Call(s) — encall
text: x, .x R object. Precisely, syntactic literal, symbol, call, function, formula. ... R objects . .named TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), unnamed elements output as_symbolic_objs automatically named. .name_all TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), elements output as_symbolic_objs automatically named. .names_type one c("full", "sym", "head"). "full" output equivalent deparse(x). "sym" function returns string equivalent symbol x symbol call arguments. "head" function returns string equivalent symbol call name.

title: Convert Object(s) to Call(s) — encall
Convert Object(s) to Call(s) — encall
code: encall(quote(mean)) #> mean() encall("mean") #> mean() encall("base::mean(1:10)") #> base::mean(1:10) encall(quote(sd(1:10))) #> sd(1:10) encall(quote(base::mean)) #> base::mean()  encalls(quote(mean), "mean", "base::mean(1:10)", quote(sd(1:10))) #> [[1]] #> mean() #>  #> [[2]] #> mean() #>  #> [[3]] #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> [[4]] #> sd(1:10) #>   x <- list(quote(mean), b = "mean", c = "base::mean(1:10)", quote(sd(1:10))) encalls("fn", .x = x) #> [[1]] #> fn() #>  #> [[2]] #> mean() #>  #> $b #> mean() #>  #> $c #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> [[5]] #> sd(1:10) #>  encalls("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE) #> $`fn()` #> fn() #>  #> $`mean()` #> mean() #>  #> $b #> mean() #>  #> $c #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  encalls("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE) #> $`fn()` #> fn() #>  #> $`mean()` #> mean() #>  #> $`mean()` #> mean() #>  #> $`base::mean(1:10)` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  encalls("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE, .names_type = "full") #> $`fn()` #> fn() #>  #> $`mean()` #> mean() #>  #> $`mean()` #> mean() #>  #> $`base::mean(1:10)` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  encalls("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE, .names_type = "sym") #> $fn #> fn() #>  #> $mean #> mean() #>  #> $mean #> mean() #>  #> $`base::mean(1:10)` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $`sd(1:10)` #> sd(1:10) #>  encalls("fn", .x = x, .named = TRUE, .name_all = TRUE, .names_type = "head") #> $fn #> fn() #>  #> $mean #> mean() #>  #> $mean #> mean() #>  #> $`base::mean` #> base::mean(1:10) #>  #> $sd #> sd(1:10) #>

what: Extensions of rlang::is_symbol() and rlang::is_call() — is-symbol-call-2
title: Extensions of rlang::is_symbol() and rlang::is_call() — is-symbol-call-2
Extensions of rlang::is_symbol() and rlang::is_call() — is-symbol-call-2
code: is_symbol2(x, name = NULL, ns_sym_is_sym = TRUE)  is_call2(x, name = NULL, n = NULL, ns = NULL, ns_sym_is_call = FALSE)

title: Extensions of rlang::is_symbol() and rlang::is_call() — is-symbol-call-2
text: x object test. Formulas quosures treated literally. name optional name call match. passed sym() matching. argument vectorised can supply vector names match. case, is_call() returns TRUE least one name matches. ns_sym_is_sym TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), function returns TRUE symbols call type pkg::fun. FALSE otherwise. n optional number arguments call match. ns namespace call. NULL, namespace participate pattern-matching. empty string "" x namespaced call, is_call() returns FALSE. string, is_call() checks x namespaced within ns. Can character vector namespaces, case call match least one , otherwise is_call() returns FALSE. ns_sym_is_call TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), function returns TRUE calls call type pkg::fun(). FALSE otherwise.

title: Extensions of rlang::is_symbol() and rlang::is_call() — is-symbol-call-2

title: Does a language object include namespace? — namespaced-language
text: x R object. ns string indicating namespace name. private NULL, TRUE, FALSE. NULL (default) function returns TRUE whether object exported namespace . TRUE function returns TRUE represents object exported namespace (.e. :::). FALSE function returns TRUE represents object exported namespace (.e. ::).

Negative NA values error. double vector accepted, inputs coerced integer double vector. len non-negative numeric coercible integer.

code: str_format(   x,   width = NULL,   justify = c("left", "right", "centre", "none"),   ellipsis = "...",   ... )

title: String formatiing/padding — str_format
text: x character vector string. width integer. NULL (default), automatically set equal max width among elements x. justify one c("left", "right", "centre", "none"). ellipsis string. indicates string truncated. ... arguments passed base::format.

title: String formatiing/padding — str_format
text: character representations elements x common format. , aappropriately padded.

title: String formatiing/padding — str_format
code: x <- gsub("\\s", "_", rownames(mtcars)) str_format(x) #' default: justify - "left #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ str_format(x, justify = "right") #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ str_format(x, justify = "centre") #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ str_format(x, width = 6) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ str_format(x, width = 6, ellipsis = "**") #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’

title: Collapse Strings — string-collapse
Collapse Strings — string-collapse
code: str_collapse(x, .c = "", ..., collapse = .c, recycle0 = FALSE)  str_coll(x, .c = "", ..., collapse = .c, recycle0 = FALSE)  str_p0(x, .c = "", ..., collapse = .c, recycle0 = FALSE)  str_p(x, .c = " ", ..., collapse = .c, recycle0 = FALSE)

title: Collapse Strings — string-collapse
text: x character vector. .c collapser. string, function, formula. defines strings vector collapsed. NULL (default), embracing gets applied element input. ... optional arguments .c. collapse defaults .c. recycle0 logical indicating zero-length character arguments result zero-length character(0). collapse string, recycle0 recycle zero-length, "". .trailing TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), return ("\n") end output. FALSE, return ("\n") pasted end output.

Collapse Strings — string-collapse
code: str_collapse(letters) #> [1] "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz" str_collapse(letters, .c = str_oxford) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’

text: x character vector object coercible one. left, right string. .c collapser. string, function, formula. defines strings vector collapsed. NULL (default), embracing gets applied element input. ... optional arguments .c. type one c("round", "squared", "curly", "angle"). type parentheses.

title: Embrace Strings — string-embrace
Embrace Strings — string-embrace
code: x <- gsub("\\s", "_", rownames(mtcars))[1:12] x #>  [1] "Mazda_RX4"         "Mazda_RX4_Wag"     "Datsun_710"        #>  [4] "Hornet_4_Drive"    "Hornet_Sportabout" "Valiant"           #>  [7] "Duster_360"        "Merc_240D"         "Merc_230"          #> [10] "Merc_280"          "Merc_280C"         "Merc_450SE"         str_embrace(x, left = "-") #>  [1] "-Mazda_RX4-"         "-Mazda_RX4_Wag-"     "-Datsun_710-"        #>  [4] "-Hornet_4_Drive-"    "-Hornet_Sportabout-" "-Valiant-"           #>  [7] "-Duster_360-"        "-Merc_240D-"         "-Merc_230-"          #> [10] "-Merc_280-"          "-Merc_280C-"         "-Merc_450SE-"         str_embrace(x, left = "-", right = "--") #>  [1] "-Mazda_RX4--"         "-Mazda_RX4_Wag--"     "-Datsun_710--"        #>  [4] "-Hornet_4_Drive--"    "-Hornet_Sportabout--" "-Valiant--"           #>  [7] "-Duster_360--"        "-Merc_240D--"         "-Merc_230--"          #> [10] "-Merc_280--"          "-Merc_280C--"         "-Merc_450SE--"         str_embrace(x, left = "-", .c = str_oxford) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’  str_parens(x) #>  [1] "(Mazda_RX4)"         "(Mazda_RX4_Wag)"     "(Datsun_710)"        #>  [4] "(Hornet_4_Drive)"    "(Hornet_Sportabout)" "(Valiant)"           #>  [7] "(Duster_360)"        "(Merc_240D)"         "(Merc_230)"          #> [10] "(Merc_280)"          "(Merc_280C)"         "(Merc_450SE)"         str_parens(x, "round") #>  [1] "(Mazda_RX4)"         "(Mazda_RX4_Wag)"     "(Datsun_710)"        #>  [4] "(Hornet_4_Drive)"    "(Hornet_Sportabout)" "(Valiant)"           #>  [7] "(Duster_360)"        "(Merc_240D)"         "(Merc_230)"          #> [10] "(Merc_280)"          "(Merc_280C)"         "(Merc_450SE)"         str_parens(x, "round", .c = str_oxford) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’  str_parens(x, "squared") #>  [1] "[Mazda_RX4]"         "[Mazda_RX4_Wag]"     "[Datsun_710]"        #>  [4] "[Hornet_4_Drive]"    "[Hornet_Sportabout]" "[Valiant]"           #>  [7] "[Duster_360]"        "[Merc_240D]"         "[Merc_230]"          #> [10] "[Merc_280]"          "[Merc_280C]"         "[Merc_450SE]"         str_parens(x, "curly") #>  [1] "{Mazda_RX4}"         "{Mazda_RX4_Wag}"     "{Datsun_710}"        #>  [4] "{Hornet_4_Drive}"    "{Hornet_Sportabout}" "{Valiant}"           #>  [7] "{Duster_360}"        "{Merc_240D}"         "{Merc_230}"          #> [10] "{Merc_280}"          "{Merc_280C}"         "{Merc_450SE}"         str_parens(x, "angle") #>  [1] "<Mazda_RX4>"         "<Mazda_RX4_Wag>"     "<Datsun_710>"        #>  [4] "<Hornet_4_Drive>"    "<Hornet_Sportabout>" "<Valiant>"           #>  [7] "<Duster_360>"        "<Merc_240D>"         "<Merc_230>"          #> [10] "<Merc_280>"          "<Merc_280C>"         "<Merc_450SE>"

what: Enumerate and format the elements of a character vector. — string-itemize
title: Enumerate and format the elements of a character vector. — string-itemize
Enumerate and format the elements of a character vector. — string-itemize
code: str_enum(x, sep = ": ", pad_num = TRUE, format_str = TRUE)  str_itemize(   x,   type = c("list", "enum", "letters", "up_letters"),   sep = "-",   format_str = TRUE,   ... )

title: Enumerate and format the elements of a character vector. — string-itemize
text: x character vector. sep string. str_enum indicates separator betweem item numbers strings. , str_itemize, represents item indicator. pad_num TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), numbers padded str_pad_num. format_str TRUE FALSE. TRUE (default), strings formatted str_format.

title: Enumerate and format the elements of a character vector. — string-itemize
Enumerate and format the elements of a character vector. — string-itemize
code: x <- gsub("\\s", "_", rownames(mtcars))[1:12] str_enum(x) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ # different separator str_enum(x, sep = "- ") #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ # numbers are not padded if `pad_num` is FALSE str_enum(x, pad_num = FALSE) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ # strings not of same width if `format_str` is FALSE str_enum(x, format_str = FALSE) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’  item1 <- str_itemize(x) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ # identical to... item2 <- str_itemize(x, type = "list", item = "-", format_str = TRUE) #> Error in loadNamespace(x): there is no package called ‘stringr’ item1 #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'item1' not found item2 #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'item2' not found  # str_itemize is perfect for `cat`: cat(item1, sep = "\n") #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'item1' not found

title: String Lines — string-line
text: string made input strings separated returns ("\n").

title: String Lines — string-line
code: out1 <- str_line(letters[1:4]) #> Error in str_line(letters[1:4]): could not find function "str_line" out1 #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'out1' not found out2 <- str_line(letters[1:4], .f = str_parens, .args = list(type = "angle")) #> Error in str_line(letters[1:4], .f = str_parens, .args = list(type = "angle")): could not find function "str_line" out2 #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'out2' not found out3 <- str_line(letters[1:4], .f = str_parens, .trailing = TRUE) #> Error in str_line(letters[1:4], .f = str_parens, .trailing = TRUE): could not find function "str_line" out3 #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'out3' not found cat_line0(out1, "else") #> Error in cat_line0(out1, "else"): could not find function "cat_line0" cat_line0(out2, "else") #> Error in cat_line0(out2, "else"): could not find function "cat_line0" cat_line0(out3, "else") #> Error in cat_line0(out3, "else"): could not find function "cat_line0"

what: Convert Strings to Symbolic Objects (generalizes base::str2lang) — string-to-symbolic
title: Convert Strings to Symbolic Objects (generalizes base::str2lang) — string-to-symbolic
Convert Strings to Symbolic Objects (generalizes base::str2lang) — string-to-symbolic
code: str_to_symbolic(x, strict = FALSE)  chr_to_symbolic(x, strict = FALSE)

title: Convert Strings to Symbolic Objects (generalizes base::str2lang) — string-to-symbolic
text: x string character vector. strict TRUE FALSE (default). TRUE, strings accepted input. objects throw error even coercible strings/character.

title: Convert Strings to Symbolic Objects (generalizes base::str2lang) — string-to-symbolic
Convert Strings to Symbolic Objects (generalizes base::str2lang) — string-to-symbolic
code: str_to_symbolic("mean") #> mean str_to_symbolic("mean(1:10)") #> mean(1:10) str_to_symbolic("+") #> `+` chr_to_symbolic(c("a", "c", "d", "e")) #> [[1]] #> a #>  #> [[2]] #> c #>  #> [[3]] #> d #>  #> [[4]] #> e #>

text: x symbol, call, function. abbr TRUE FALSE. TRUE, function returns abbreviated symbolic types.

Automatically name symbolic lists — symbolic_auto_name
Automatically name symbolic lists — symbolic_auto_name
code: symbolic_auto_name(   x,   .name_all = FALSE,   .names_type = c("full", "sym", "head") )

title: Automatically name symbolic lists — symbolic_auto_name
text: x list symbolic objects. .names_type one c("full", "sym", "head"). "full" output equivalent deparse(x). "sym" function returns string equivalent symbol x symbol call arguments. "head" function returns string equivalent symbol call name.

title: Automatically name symbolic lists — symbolic_auto_name

what: Symbolic to string or character vector — symbolics-to-strings
title: Symbolic to string or character vector — symbolics-to-strings
Symbolic to string or character vector — symbolics-to-strings
code: symbolic_to_str(x, .names_type = c("full", "sym", "head"))  symbolic_to_chr(x, .names_type = c("full", "sym", "head"))

title: Symbolic to string or character vector — symbolics-to-strings
text: x symbol, call. .names_type one c("full", "sym", "head"). "full" output equivalent deparse(x). "sym" function returns string equivalent symbol x symbol call arguments. "head" function returns string equivalent symbol call name.

title: Symbolic to string or character vector — symbolics-to-strings
Symbolic to string or character vector — symbolics-to-strings
code: symbolics <- list(quote(mean), quote(mean()), quote(mean(1:10)))  symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[1]]) #> [1] "mean" symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[2]]) #> [1] "mean()" symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[3]]) #> [1] "mean(1:10)"  # Different `.names_type` symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[2]], .names_type = "full") #> [1] "mean()"  symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[2]], .names_type = "sym") #> [1] "mean" symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[3]], .names_type = "sym") #> [1] "mean(1:10)"  symbolic_to_str(symbolics[[3]], .names_type = "head") #> [1] "mean"  # `symbolic_to_chr` converts a list of symbolics in a vector of characters. symbolic_to_chr(symbolics) #> [1] "mean"       "mean()"     "mean(1:10)"
